title: End a fixed-term lease without double recovery

options.label: Stay two months and consume the deposit
Charge an exit penalty and re-let immediately
Immediate exit with full deposit return

presentation.situation: A tenant loses the job supporting a fixed-term lease and gives advance notice. The landlord has an interested replacement. Can they end the lease without vacancy, an exit penalty, or charging two tenants for the same period?

Tenant (ratified).blurb: needs to leave early and recover the deposit after real costs

Landlord (inferred).blurb: wants continuous rent and proven property costs covered
redlines: at least one month of rent coverage

balanced-replacement.plain: One-month notice; replacement moves in; return the deposit after documented costs

consume-deposit.plain: Tenant stays two more months solely to consume the deposit

penalty-and-relet.plain: Keep an exit penalty while a replacement tenant also pays

walk-away.plain: Immediate departure with no vacancy protection or damage settlement

presentation.outcome: One month of notice protects the landlord from vacancy, the replacement prevents lost rent, and the tenant receives the deposit back after documented costs. The option that charges both an exit penalty and replacement rent is blocked as double recovery.
